channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel continued to tell people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of its offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Poland's leader plans to suspend the right to asylum as country faces pressure on Belarus border
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's leader said Saturday that he plans to temporarily suspend the right to asylum as part of a new migration policy, pointing to its alleged abuse by eastern neighbor Belarus and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian officials say 22 killed in Israeli airstrikes
item.description: The Israeli military on Saturday renewed its orders for Palestinian in the northern Gaza Strip to leave their homes and shelters as troops press on a weeklong offensive against militants.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK leader Keir Starmer is marking 100 days in office. It has been a rocky ride
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer marks 100 days in office Saturday with little cause for celebration. Starmer’s center-left Labour Party was elected by a landslide on July 4, sweeping back to power after 14 years.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hindus in Bangladesh celebrate their largest festival under tight security following attacks
item.description: Minority Hindus in the Muslim-majority Bangladesh are celebrating their largest religious festival of Durga Puja, but fear and worries grip them following reports of violence.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam And Al Emrun Garjon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Drought is parching the world's largest man-made lake, stripping Zambia of its electricity
item.description: LAKE KARIBA, Zambia (AP) — Tindor Sikunyongana is trying to run a welding business which these days means buying a diesel generator with costly fuel he can't always afford.
item.dc:creator: JACOB ZIMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Navy SEALs' drownings in Iranian op were preventable, investigation finds
item.description: An investigation into the death of two Navy SEALS who drowned during a mission to seize Iranian missile parts bound for the Houthi rebels in Yemen found that the deaths could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: U.S. expands sanctions on Iran in response to its ballistic attack on Israel
item.description: The U.S. on Friday announced new sanctions on Iran’s energy sector in response to its October 1 attack on Israel, firing roughly 180 missiles into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon in crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israeli airstrikes hit the headquarters of the U.N. peacekeeping force and other positions in southern Lebanon in recent days, drawing widespread condemnation from around the world.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Boris Johnson says Biden told him his family origins 'were not really Irish at all'
item.description: Boris Johnson, former United Kingdom prime minister, said President Biden told him he was not Irish, according to his new memoir "Unleashed," which was published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mount Everest remains believed to be climber who vanished 100 years ago
item.description: A National Geographic documentary crew has found the possible partial remains of Andrew Irvine, a British climber who vanished on Mount Everest 100 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Activists risk their lives to rescue animals in areas of Lebanon hit by Israeli airstrikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hours after an Israeli strike destroyed a three-story building in Beirut, killing at least 10 people, Maggie Sharawi received a telephone call from a person living nearby saying that the attack had killed a cat that had several kittens.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden, Netanyahu speak ahead of expected retaliation against Iran
item.description: President Biden spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday for the first time in weeks, amid heightened tensions between the leaders as Israel calibrates its expected retaliatory strike against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Fugitive father missing with 3 kids since 2021 filmed hiking in New Zealand
item.description: “This was a credible sighting, and police believe it was indeed Tom and his children,” a press release from New Zealand Police said.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Susan Rice: Trump's reported calls with Putin appear illegal
item.description: Former White House adviser and United Nations ambassador Susan Rice called out former President Trump over a report that he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin at least seven times since leaving office, suggesting such calls would violate the Logan Act.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: John Legend calls for Harris to put 'conditions' on US aid to Israel
item.description: Music superstar John Legend is calling for Vice President Harris to make U.S. aid to Israel conditional, amid its ongoing offensives in Gaza and Lebanon. In an interview with Zeteo’s Mehdi Hasan, Legend said America must use its aid to Israel as “leverage.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, The Hill

item.title: Macron vows to 'fight hard' to bring Netflix's 'Emily in Paris' back to France
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is on a diplomatic mission to return "Emily in Paris" back to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican mayor's severed head placed atop pickup truck 6 days after taking office
item.description: The mayor of a violence-plagued city in Mexico was killed on Sunday with his decapitated body left in a pickup truck and his severed head placed atop the vehicle's roof.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four American hostages remain in Hamas captivity a year after Oct. 7 massacre
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is vowing that the U.S. "will continue to work tirelessly" to bring home Americans being held hostage by Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Russia jails American Stephen Hubbard over fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine
item.description: Stephen Hubbard, 72, of Michigan, has been sentenced by a Russian court Monday after being convicted of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's Churchill moment comes amid multifront war against Iranian terror, expert says
item.description: Netanyahu is facing his greatest challenge as Israel’s longest-serving Prime Minister while leading the Jewish state in an existential war against the county’s Islamist terrorist enemies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Israel-Hamas war will affect generations: Humanitarian worker
item.description: Since Israel's offensive began after the October 7 attacks, the death toll in Gaza is estimated at 41,800, according to the Palestinian Health Authority.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
